title: Grant Base
summary: A secretary-desk base cabinet with a center drawer divider, two false-fronted doors, and a jigsaw-cut footer — pairs with the Grant Secretary Hutch.
description:
This base is built as one wide plywood case split by a center divider into a bank of drawers on one side and open cubby storage (or a second set of drawers) on the other, with two doors below hiding additional storage. The divider isn't just a partition — building it in early, screwed to both the top and bottom shelf, is what keeps the whole case from racking, and it gives the drawer bank a second reference surface beyond the outer sides.

The doors use a false-front technique instead of frame-and-panel construction: cut a flat plywood door blank to fit the opening with a small 1/16"–1/8" gap, then glue strips of 1/4" plywood to its face as applied trim, which reads as a real door frame without any actual joinery. Fit every door and drawer in its opening before attaching any face — center each with an even gap on all sides, then let the face's position follow the box, not a fixed measurement.

tools.note: Holds the applied door trim strips while the glue sets.

cutList.note: Mark and jigsaw a decorative profile before attaching.
Cut to fit your actual door openings, leaving 1/16"–1/8" gap.
Cut to fit each door face — glued on as applied trim, not structural framing.
Size to your openings, leaving 1/2" clearance per side for the drawer slides.
Inset to the drawer sides rather than sitting under them.

steps.title: Cut every panel to size
steps.body: Cut the divider sides, drawer box divider pieces, bottom shelf, top, and sides from your two sheets of 3/4" plywood or MDF. Doors, drawers, and moulding all get cut to fit later, once real openings exist to measure against.

steps.title: Build the center divider
steps.body: Assemble the divider sides and drawer box divider pieces into the center drawer divider unit. Adding internal shelves here adds weight but also real structure and a better-aligned drawer box — worth doing since you already have the material from the two full sheets.

steps.title: Attach the top, bottom, and divider
steps.body: Mark out the top and bottom shelf locations and attach the center divider to both with 2" screws and glue (or pocket-hole screws and glue).

steps.title: Cut the side profiles
steps.body: Mark the sides as shown in your reference photos and cut with a jigsaw — note the side cutout profile is different from the front footer cutout, so don't reuse one template for both.

steps.title: Attach the front footer
steps.body: Attach the footer blank to the bottom, inset as shown in your reference. Mark and cut the decorative profile with a jigsaw — cutting it in place (rather than off the cabinet) avoids extra clamping if you're confident with the saw. A nailer with 1-1/4" or 2" finish nails and glue works well here.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back with 1-1/4" screws or nails and glue, making sure to fasten into the shelves and divider as well as the sides — this is what locks the whole case square. Check it's perfectly square before moving on.

steps.title: Fit and attach the moulding
steps.body: Cut the moulding to fit precisely: start with the center piece (mitered 45° on both ends) and get it right first, then cut the side pieces to fit against it. Expect to make several test cuts before you're satisfied — moulding fit is worth the extra time since it's the most visible trim on the piece.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Measure each door opening and cut a 3/4" plywood or MDF scrap door blank to fit, leaving about 1/16"–1/8" gap around it (adjust based on your hinge type). Once you're happy with the fit, cut 1/4" plywood into 2-1/2"-wide strips and glue and clamp them to the door face as applied trim, keeping corners securely clamped — but not so tight that all the glue squeezes out. Build two doors.

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Cut drawer box parts from 3/4" plywood strips at 7-1/4" wide. Cut drawer bottoms from leftover panel stock, inset to the sides rather than sitting flush under them, and nail or screw through the sides into the bottoms. Always measure your actual drawer box before building it — standard slides need 1/2" clearance on each side. Don't attach the drawer face until the box is fully installed in the cabinet, so you can position it floating with an equal gap on all sides.

steps.title: Finish and add hardware
steps.body: Remove the doors and drawers. Fill any exposed holes and plywood edges with wood filler. Sand, apply edge banding to any exposed plywood edges if staining, or prime and paint as desired. Reinstall the doors and drawers and add the hinges and knobs.

images.alt: Secretary desk base cabinet with drawers and two false-front doors
